g.data-narration: Welcome to Threaded Binary Trees! Today we'll explore how to eliminate wasted NULL pointers and enable efficient O(1) space traversal.
In a binary tree with n nodes, we have 2n pointer fields, but n+1 of them are NULL—that's more than half the pointers wasted!
g.text: In a binary tree with n nodes:
Total pointer fields: 2n | Used pointers: n-1 | NULL pointers: n+1
Proof: 2n - (n-1) = n+1. For 100 nodes, 101 out of 200 pointers are NULL!

g.data-narration: The solution: replace NULL pointers with threads pointing to inorder predecessor or successor. This eliminates wasted memory and enables stack-free traversal!
g.text: Replace NULL pointers with threads to inorder predecessor/successor
Instead of NULL, store useful navigation information for traversal

g.data-narration: There are three types: right-threaded replaces right NULLs, left-threaded replaces left NULLs, and fully-threaded replaces both for maximum efficiency.
g.text: Three Types of Threaded Binary Trees

g.data-narration: A regular node has 3 fields, but a threaded node adds two boolean flags—lthread and rthread—to distinguish threads from real child pointers.
Thread flags tell us whether a pointer is a real child or a thread. When lthread=1, left points to predecessor; when rthread=1, right points to successor.
g.text: Thread Flags: Distinguishing Threads from Real Pointers

g.data-narration: The header node is a sentinel that handles boundary cases—leftmost node's predecessor and rightmost node's successor both point to the header.
g.text: The Header Node: Sentinel for Boundary Cases

g.data-narration: Let's use this example tree. The inorder traversal is D, B, E, A, C, F, G—we'll thread each node's NULL pointers to their inorder neighbors.
g.text: D → B → E → A → C → F → G
These will be the thread destinations!

g.data-narration: Here's the fully threaded tree! D threads to Header and B, E threads to B and A, and so on. Dashed purple lines show the thread connections.
g.text: Fully Threaded Binary Tree (with threads shown)

g.data-narration: Here's the complete thread flag table for our example. Notice that nodes with no left child have lthread=1, and nodes with no right child have rthread=1.
The key advantage: inorder traversal without stack or recursion! Just follow threads—go leftmost, visit node, then follow right thread to successor.
g.text: Inorder Traversal: No Stack, No Recursion!

g.data-narration: Here's the C implementation. The inorder successor function checks if rthread is 1—if so, return right directly. Otherwise, go right then find leftmost.
g.text: return node->right; // Thread points to successor
// Else go right, then find leftmost

g.data-narration: Threaded trees save O(1) space during traversal and find successors in O(1), but cost extra memory for flags and complicate insertion and deletion.
Key takeaways: n+1 NULL pointers become threads, three types exist, header handles boundaries, and traversal needs O(1) space instead of O(h) stack.
g.text: Threaded Binary Trees: Memory efficient + Fast inorder traversal
